title: End-date field is only relevant when the matching ongoing flag is 'N'
description: When a form has both an *ENDAT field and a matching *ONGO field (e.g. AEENDAT+AEONGO, CMENDAT+CMONGO, MHENDAT+MHONGO), the end-date field gets `relevant: ${<base>ONGO}='N'` — the end-date is shown only when the corresponding condition/event is reported as not-ongoing.
rationale: While a condition is ongoing there is no end date to record. Coordinators forced to enter a placeholder value (today's date, 1900-01-01, etc.) generate dirty data that complicates downstream analysis. Conditional relevance on the ongoing flag makes the form behave intuitively and keeps the dataset clean.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} is an end-date paired with an ongoing-flag sibling. Verify relevant attribute is `${<sibling>ONGO}='N'`.

effect.soft: Emit relevant: `${<base>ONGO}='N'` where <base> is the field-name prefix shared by the ONGO sibling (e.g. for AEENDAT, the sibling is AEONGO and relevant references ${AEONGO}). Like 7A, sibling-name interpolation is not yet expressible in the DSL — handled here in soft. The relevance gate pairs with the 7A constraint cleanly: end-date appears only when ongoing='N', and the value must be after the start date.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7C during Phase B.1c-final. Same sibling-interpolation DSL gap as 7A. Pairs with 7A (paired_start_end_date_constraint) — when both fire together on an ENDAT field, the result is a field that's only visible when ongoing='N' AND has a constraint linking it to the matching STDAT.
